definition: The process of learning and gaining knowledge and skills.
examples: Education helps people find better jobs.
Every child deserves a good education.
She received her education in Ireland.
Education can change people's lives.
He works in the field of education.
notes: Usually uncountable. We say 'an education' when talking about a particular type of education.

definition: The act of traveling from one place to another or a process of personal development.
examples: Our English journey starts today.
The journey took nearly six hours.
They went on a long journey across Europe.
Learning a language is a lifelong journey.
She shared her journey of becoming a doctor.
notes: Can be used literally (travel) or figuratively (life experience, learning process).

definition: Fair treatment of people according to what is right or lawful.
examples: He dedicated his life to fighting for justice.
The victims demanded justice.
Everyone should be treated with justice and respect.
The court seeks justice for all citizens.
She has a strong sense of justice.

definition: Kindness and forgiveness shown toward someone whom you could punish.
examples: Volunteer work requires mercy and compassion.
The prisoner asked for mercy.
They showed mercy to the defeated enemy.
May Allah have mercy on him.
The judge decided to show mercy.
notes: Common in religious and legal contexts.

definition: A feeling of sympathy and a desire to help people who are suffering.
examples: Volunteer work requires mercy and compassion.
She treated her patients with compassion.
Compassion is one of his greatest qualities.
Children need love and compassion.
He showed compassion toward the homeless.

definition: Good for your health or morally good and positive.
examples: My mother always buys fresh and wholesome food.
The children enjoyed a wholesome family environment.
They try to eat wholesome meals.
It is a wholesome activity for young people.
She prefers wholesome ingredients.
notes: Often used with food, lifestyle, and family activities.

definition: The careful study of a subject to discover new information.
examples: We did a small research project in class.
The company is conducting market research.
She spent years doing research on climate change.
More research is needed in this area.
The findings are based on recent research.
notes: Usually uncountable. We say 'do research' rather than 'make research'.

definition: Deep respect for God and a sincere desire to do what is right.
examples: Piety is shown not only in worship but also in good character.
His piety inspired many people around him.
She tried to live a life of piety and kindness.
Many people admire her piety and humility.
Piety should be reflected in both words and actions.
notes: Often used in religious contexts to describe devotion and righteous behavior.

definition: Deserving respect because of honesty, integrity, or good actions.
examples: He is an honorable man who always keeps his promises.
Helping others is an honorable thing to do.
She made an honorable decision.
Everyone respected his honorable behavior.
It is honorable to admit your mistakes.

definition: A large amount of money, possessions, or valuable resources.
examples: Wealth alone does not guarantee happiness.
He accumulated great wealth through his business.
Good health is a form of wealth.
She inherited a considerable amount of wealth.
Some people spend their lives chasing wealth.

definition: Morally good and fair, especially according to religious or ethical principles.
examples: He tried to live a righteous life.
A righteous person treats others with honesty and mercy.
She made a righteous decision even though it was difficult.
Many people respected him for his righteous character.
Righteous actions should come from sincere intentions.
notes: Often used in religious or moral contexts. It can describe a person, action, or way of life.

definition: A person; a member of the human race.
examples: Every human being deserves respect.
A human being needs food, water, and care.
She believes every human being has value.
We should treat each human being with kindness.
No human being is perfect.

definition: All space, time, matter, and energy, including all stars, planets, and galaxies.
examples: The universe is incredibly vast.
Scientists study how the universe began.
There are billions of galaxies in the universe.
Some people look at the universe and think about its Creator.
We still know very little about the universe.

definition: Strong belief or trust in God, a religion, a person, or an idea.
examples: Faith gives many people strength.
She has strong faith in Allah.
He never lost faith during difficult times.
Their faith taught them to be patient and kind.
I have faith that things will improve.
notes: Can mean religious belief or trust in someone or something.

definition: Before a particular time or event.
examples: Please let me know beforehand if you cannot come.
We booked the tickets beforehand.
It is better to prepare everything beforehand.
She told me beforehand that she might be late.
You should check the requirements beforehand.
notes: Similar to 'in advance'. Usually placed at the end of a sentence.

translation: на протяжении, в течение всего, по всему
definition: During the whole period of time or in every part of a place.
examples: She stayed calm throughout the interview.
It rained throughout the night.
The company has offices throughout Europe.
He worked hard throughout the course.
There were small improvements throughout the year.
notes: Can refer to time or place. It often means 'from the beginning to the end of something'.

definition: Physical or mental energy used to do something.
examples: Learning English takes time and effort.
She put a lot of effort into the project.
Your effort is more important than perfection.
He made an effort to arrive on time.
The team achieved success through hard effort.
notes: Common phrases: make an effort, put effort into something, with effort.
